pool.baseline:
hey can u summarize the main pros and cons of this new energy bill? i saw a headline about it but it was kinda long. here is the text: 

"The Pennsylvania State Energy Transition Act (SETA) proposes a significant shift in state subsidy allocations. Under the bill, $500 million currently earmarked for traditional coal extraction and processing facilities would be redirected toward a new 'Solar Infrastructure Fund.' Supporters of the bill argue that this is a necessary step to modernize the state's grid and meet emission targets, noting that the solar sector has grown by 20% in the last two years. Opponents, including several labor unions from the coal regions, argue that the bill will lead to immediate job losses in traditional energy sectors and that the solar industry cannot yet provide the same level of reliable, baseload power required for the state."
